destinations.whyGo: The Olympic capital and Vaud's lively university city — terraced lakefront, the Olympic Museum, a vibrant Flon nightlife district and the most spectacular metro climb in Europe (M2). Excellent springboard to the Lavaux vineyards.
highlights: M2 metro descent from the cathedral to Ouchy
Springboard to the Lavaux UNESCO vineyards

destinations.whyGo: Riviera-feel resort at the head of Lake Geneva, home to the Montreux Jazz Festival, the Chillon Castle peninsula and the lakeside Freddie Mercury statue. Pairs perfectly with a stop in the Lavaux vineyards.
highlights: Lavaux vineyards on the way back

destinations.whyGo: Storybook canal town nicknamed 'the Venice of the Alps,' with a turquoise lake, pastel canalside houses and the medieval Palais de l'Île. One of the most scenic day trips in France.
highlights: Climbing the château for old town views

destinations.whyGo: France's flagship Alpine resort directly beneath Mont Blanc. The Aiguille du Midi cable car takes you to 3,842 m for one of Europe's most thrilling viewpoints. Year-round destination — skiing in winter, hiking and climbing in summer.

directBus.frequency: Several daily via FlixBus, Mountain Drop-offs

highlights: Mer de Glace glacier and train
Skiing in winter (Les Houches, Brevent, Grands Montets)
Hiking the Tour du Mont Blanc in summer

destinations.whyGo: A perfectly preserved medieval village on the French shore of Lake Geneva, classified one of the 'most beautiful villages of France.' Flower-draped stone houses, the Jardin des Cinq Sens, and the easiest possible international boat-trip from Geneva.
highlights: Combine with Nyon on the same boat-loop

destinations.whyGo: Switzerland's largest city and financial capital — Kunsthaus art, the Bahnhofstrasse luxury shopping mile, riverside swimming, and an energetic post-industrial nightlife scene in Zürich West.

Switzerland's capital and a UNESCO-listed medieval Old Town wrapped in a loop of the river Aare. Famous for the Zytglogge clock tower, six kilometres of arcaded shopping streets, the Bear Park, and (in summer) the local ritual of floating down the Aare.

France's gastronomic capital and a UNESCO World Heritage city, with the Renaissance traboules of Vieux Lyon, the Roman amphitheatres of Fourvière, and the bouchon bistro tradition that defines French comfort cooking.
highlights: Basilica of Fourvière and panoramic terrace
Les Halles de Lyon Paul Bocuse food hall

destinations.whyGo: Italian-speaking Ticino — palm trees, lakes, and three UNESCO-listed medieval castles in Bellinzona. A taste of Italy without leaving Switzerland.

directTrain.journeyTime: Approx. 4h15 (via Zurich or via the Gotthard base tunnel)

destinations.whyGo: The launchpad for the Jungfrau region — Jungfraujoch (the 'Top of Europe' at 3,454 m), Lauterbrunnen's valley of 72 waterfalls, and the Schilthorn revolving restaurant. Switzerland's signature mountain experience.

directTrain.journeyTime: Approx. 2h45 to Interlaken Ost (one change at Bern)

destinations.whyGo: Belle Époque French spa town on the south shore of Lake Geneva, famous for its bottled mineral water, casino, lakeside palace hotels and a thermal spa complex. The easiest international ferry day-trip from Geneva.

directBus.stationInGeneva: Geneva Mont-Blanc Pier (CGN boats) or via Lausanne ferry
directBus.journeyTime: Approx. 2h35 by lake (or via Lausanne short ferry from Ouchy)

highlights: Funicular up to Évian's upper town
Combine with Lausanne via the cross-lake ferry

destinations.whyGo: 30 km of UNESCO-listed terraced vineyards plunging down to Lake Geneva, built over eight centuries. Walking the Chemin du Lavaux is among the most spectacular short hikes in Europe.

directTrain.journeyTime: Approx. 50–55 minutes to Lutry, Cully or Epesses

highlights: Lavaux Vinorama wine centre in Rivaz

destinations.whyGo: Hilltop walled village in Fribourg canton, home to the Gruyère cheese tradition and the offbeat HR Giger Alien-art museum. Compact, photogenic and perfect for a half-day.
highlights: Cailler chocolate factory in nearby Broc
